title: Realtime Harness Dashboard
subtitle: Make active lifecycle runs visible while inference units are still running.
scope: Standalone living-doc harness dashboard, WebSocket event stream, lifecycle graph updates, contract handoffs, and inference-unit log inspection.
sourceCoverage: Created from the May 10, 2026 objective-shaping discussion about making the standalone harness dashboard realtime via WebSockets, including live diagram updates, the correct inference-unit log stream for the selected running unit, and contract-selected MCP/tool profiles for each inference unit.
objective: Build the standalone living-doc harness dashboard into a real-time operator surface for active lifecycle runs: the backend must stream lifecycle, inference-unit, contract, graph, artifact, and log events over WebSockets; the frontend must update the lifecycle graph and selected detail panes without manual refresh; and an operator must be able to see which inference unit is currently running, what contract started it, what MCP/tool profile constrained it, what log/evidence stream is available for that unit, and how each emitted event changes the graph or lifecycle state. The system must preserve the existing standalone/local-only boundary and must not depend on the supervising user chat session for state, decisions, tools, or logs.
successCondition: A standalone harness run can be watched live in the local dashboard: graph cards and contract arrows appear or change status from streamed artifacts, the active inference unit is highlighted, the side pane shows the contract, MCP/tool profile, and artifact paths that started that unit, the log pane tails the matching local inference-unit log, reload reconstructs the same graph and recent event history from persisted artifacts, blocker/error states remain inspectable, and automated contract/UI tests prove the stream, graph, log selection, recovery, contract-selected tool profile, and local-only boundaries.

runState.nextObjectiveAction: Let the standalone lifecycle controller consume the source objectiveReady state, green acceptance criteria, rendered document, native trace, evidence bundle, and controller-owned proof-route artifacts, then run its owned reviewer and closure-review path before classifying the lifecycle as closed.

basis: Objective conservation preserved all terms: WebSocket lifecycle/inference/contract/graph/artifact/log streaming is contract-proven and the actual dashboard script now has executable realtime UI-runtime proof for graph/detail/log updates without reload.

The dashboard now fetches persisted /api/lifecycles/:id/events history before opening the live WebSocket, so reload reconstructs recent event rows and cached selected-unit log tails from local artifacts rather than waiting on frontend memory.
The current worker environment can bind localhost, launch Chromium, and run tests/e2e/harness-dashboard-realtime.spec.mjs successfully; the prior local-browser-blocked evidence is superseded.
The lifecycle now has a deterministic controller-owned proof route that writes typed proof artifacts before reviewer and closure units judge the run.

Contract-bound inference units now carry a toolProfile contract; the default local-harness/local-repair profiles start Codex with --ignore-user-config, explicitly pass --sandbox danger-full-access, and allowlist only the local living_doc_compositor MCP server.

The dashboard lifecycle start path now forwards --execute-proof-routes and --tool-profile local-harness, so runs started from the local operator surface use the same controller-owned proof route and tool-profile contract as direct CLI runs.
Iteration 2 reran the focused realtime dashboard contract set, the Playwright browser proof, and npm run test:contract successfully from the standalone local worker profile.

Iteration 2 resolves objectiveReady=true at the source living-doc level because all acceptance criteria are complete, the rendered standalone document exists, the native trace and evidence bundle are present, and the controller-owned browser proof route passed with localhost and Chromium capability checks.

Catalytic repair reconciled the reviewer/handover contradiction: those artifacts captured an earlier proof-gate view with 13 unproven criteria, while the inspected worker trace and current source doc show the criteria now have local contract/UI/browser proof but still lack controller-owned closure.
Reaction-path validation still blocks classifying the lifecycle as closed inside the worker process; the nearest honest stage is objective-ready with controller closure review pending.

supervisorMay: create issues and tickets from this doc
start or watch the standalone lifecycle run
inspect local dashboard, run artifacts, and streamed evidence
supervisorMustNot: treat a static mockup as completion
claim live reasoning visibility without inspecting the selected unit's emitted local log stream
use the supervising user chat session as the source of lifecycle state
close the objective because WebSockets connect while graph, contract, log, or recovery proof is missing

supersededBlockedCommands.blocker: Configured Python web server failed to bind 127.0.0.1:4173 with PermissionError: [Errno 1] Operation not permitted.
Chromium launched but exited with MachPortRendezvousServer bootstrap_check_in Permission denied (1100).
Node HTTP bind to 127.0.0.1 also fails with listen EPERM in this worker.

sourceChanges: scripts/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.mjs now emits explicit lifecycle_started, inference_unit_started, inference_unit_finished, lifecycle_closed, lifecycle_error, and lifecycle_blocked events derived from persisted local graph/lifecycle artifacts.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs asserts WebSocket/history event coverage, local-only privacy, blocker events, selected node tails, and lifecycle_closed recovery.
tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-artifact-graph.spec.mjs now proves repair-path and no-repair lifecycle graph shapes differ and no repair nodes appear for a closable run.

tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-realtime-ui.spec.mjs evaluates the actual dashboard script with mocked fetch/WebSocket events and proves active-unit highlighting, contract paths, graph arrow creation, selected log switching, persisted event-history restoration, live event listing, and no reload.

scripts/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.mjs now loads /api/lifecycles/:id/events during selected-graph initialization before connecting the WebSocket, restoring recent event rows and cached log tails from persisted local artifacts on reload.

scripts/living-doc-harness-proof-route.mjs adds controller-owned deterministic proof routes with local capability probes, typed pass/fail/blocked artifacts, stdout/stderr paths, and closure contribution metadata.
scripts/living-doc-harness-lifecycle.mjs can run doc-declared proof routes with --execute-proof-routes between worker completion and reviewer/finalization.

scripts/living-doc-harness-tool-profile.mjs defines contract-selected inference tool profiles; local-harness and local-repair launch Codex with --ignore-user-config, --sandbox danger-full-access, and only the local living_doc_compositor MCP override.
scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.mjs writes the selected toolProfile into prompt, input contract, result, and events before starting headless Codex.
scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs applies the local-harness profile to worker inference startup so the initial unit no longer inherits ambient OAuth-backed MCP tools.

scripts/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.mjs forwards executeProofRoutes and toolProfile from /api/lifecycles to the standalone lifecycle process and exposes node-level toolProfile metadata in the graph inspector.
tests/e2e/harness-dashboard-realtime.spec.mjs now runs as a real Playwright proof on the Mac by serving the dashboard through a routed page URL instead of setContent on about:blank.

docs/living-doc-harness-realtime-dashboard.json now records objectiveReady=true while leaving lifecycle closure to the controller-owned reviewer and closure-review path, so source evidence derivation can satisfy the pending acceptanceCriteriaSatisfied gate without the worker running prohibited controller commands.

proofRoutes.description: Controller-owned deterministic browser proof for the realtime dashboard. This runs outside the worker inference unit so localhost binding and Chromium launch are tested by the lifecycle controller environment.

objectiveFacets.name: Structured WebSocket stream
objectiveFacets.description: Lifecycle, inference-unit, contract, graph, artifact, and log events stream from the backend while a run is active.

objectiveFacets.name: Live operator surface
objectiveFacets.description: The dashboard updates graph and detail panes without refresh and makes the active unit inspectable.

objectiveFacets.name: Correct inference-unit log inspection
objectiveFacets.description: Selecting a running or completed unit shows the local log stream for that exact unit, not a stale or global log.

objectiveFacets.name: Artifact-derived graph
objectiveFacets.description: Graph nodes and contract arrows are generated from streamed and persisted lifecycle artifacts, not hard-coded frontend assumptions.

objectiveFacets.name: Reload recovery
objectiveFacets.description: Reloading the dashboard reconstructs current lifecycle state, graph, contracts, and recent log tail from persisted local evidence.

objectiveFacets.name: Standalone local-only boundary
objectiveFacets.description: The implementation reads local harness artifacts and logs only and does not depend on supervising chat state or remote telemetry.

objectiveFacets.name: Contract-selected MCP/tool profile
objectiveFacets.description: Each inference unit starts with an explicit tool profile from its contract, including sandbox mode and local MCP allowlist, so local units do not inherit ambient OAuth-backed MCPs or plugins and do retain the write privileges needed to satisfy the objective.

invariants.statement: The WebSocket stream transports lifecycle evidence and state changes; it does not become the semantic authority for objective closure.
A displayed inference log is valid only when it is selected from the artifact path of the graph node or contract currently being inspected.
Graph nodes, arrows, labels, and statuses must derive from lifecycle artifacts or streamed events that are persisted as local evidence.
The dashboard may show emitted logs, prompts, contracts, tool calls, stdout, stderr, summaries, and artifacts, but must not claim access to hidden model reasoning that is not emitted.
The realtime dashboard must operate from local standalone harness artifacts and must not rely on the supervising chat session for lifecycle state, decisions, or logs.
An inference unit's MCP/tool surface and sandbox mode must come from its launch contract and recorded toolProfile, not from the supervising session or broad ambient Codex user configuration.

sections.title: Acceptance Criteria
sections.rationale: Defines the objective-tight closure gates for making the dashboard a live operator surface rather than a static graph or WebSocket demo.

data.name: Active lifecycle runs emit a structured WebSocket event stream
data.objectiveTerm: backend must stream lifecycle, inference-unit, contract, graph, artifact, and log events over WebSockets
data.criterion: Active lifecycle runs emit typed WebSocket events for lifecycle start, unit start, contract handoff, graph update, artifact update, log append, unit finish, lifecycle close, and lifecycle error or blocker.
data.proofRequired: A local WebSocket endpoint, event schema, and captured test run or replay fixture showing each required event type.
data.acceptanceTest: Automated contract test connects to the WebSocket endpoint or replay harness and asserts event schema, event type coverage, runId/lifecycleId linkage, and stable event ordering for a normal run.
data.proofStatus: Full contract suite passes. Dashboard server spec asserts WebSocket endpoint, event schema, lifecycle_started, inference_unit_started, inference_unit_finished, contract_handoff, graph_update, artifact_update, log_append, lifecycle_closed, and lifecycle_blocked coverage from local artifacts.

data.name: Dashboard updates without manual refresh
data.objectiveTerm: frontend must update the lifecycle graph and selected detail panes without manual refresh
data.criterion: While a run is active, graph cards, arrows, statuses, and detail panes update in place from streamed events without requiring a page reload or Refresh click.
data.proofRequired: Browser test starts or replays a lifecycle stream and observes graph/detail DOM changes without triggering reload or manual refresh.
data.acceptanceTest: Playwright or equivalent test opens the dashboard, streams a fixture run, and asserts a node and arrow appear or change status while the document location and reload count remain unchanged.
data.proofStatus: Realtime UI contract evaluates the actual dashboard script, restores persisted event history, streams graph_update and log_append events through a mocked WebSocket, asserts graph/detail DOM changes and reloadCount=0. Playwright realtime browser proof now passes in this worker environment.

data.name: Lifecycle graph is generated from streamed lifecycle artifacts
data.objectiveTerm: how each emitted event changes the graph or lifecycle state
data.criterion: The live graph renders the actual units and contract handoffs present in streamed and persisted artifacts, including both a no-repair run and a repair-path run.
data.proofRequired: Two lifecycle fixture streams with different shapes and assertions that graph nodes/edges match the event/artifact payload, not a hard-coded frontend sequence.
data.acceptanceTest: Graph contract streams one closable run and one repairable run; the rendered graph must differ according to events and must include no repair nodes for the closable run.
data.proofStatus: Artifact graph contract now covers both repair-path and closable no-repair lifecycle fixtures and asserts graph shape comes from artifacts, not a fixed frontend sequence.

data.name: Currently running inference unit is visible and inspectable
data.objectiveTerm: operator must be able to see which inference unit is currently running
data.criterion: The active inference unit is highlighted in the graph and selectable in the side pane with role, iteration, status, contract path, artifact paths, and available log stream.
data.proofRequired: Simulated or real active-unit event stream and UI test proving the active card, status, and side-pane fields update while the unit is running.
data.acceptanceTest: Stream a unit-start event followed by delayed log events; before unit-finish, the dashboard must mark that unit active and show its input contract and artifact path references.
data.proofStatus: Server graph exposes activeInferenceUnitId and realtime UI contract proves the dashboard marks the active card as current-unit and shows the selected unit's contract/artifact paths in the inspector.

data.name: Dashboard shows the right inference log for the selected unit
data.objectiveTerm: what log/evidence stream is available for that unit
data.criterion: Selecting an active or completed inference-unit card tails the local log or event file recorded for that unit, and changing selection switches to the newly selected unit's stream.
data.proofRequired: Fixture with at least two unit logs containing distinguishable lines and a UI/API assertion that the displayed lines come from the selected unit artifact path.
data.acceptanceTest: Select worker, assert worker-only log marker; select reviewer, assert reviewer-only log marker and absence of worker-only marker.
data.proofStatus: Server/API contract proves selected graph-node tail reads the node artifact path; realtime UI contract proves worker-only and reviewer-only marker switching and absence of stale worker log after reviewer selection.

data.name: Contract handoffs are visible and inspectable in real time
data.criterion: Streamed handoff events create or update graph arrows with labels, and selecting an arrow shows contract gate, effect, status, source unit, target unit, artifact paths, and commitIntent or repair-order data when present.
data.proofRequired: Contract-handoff fixture and UI/API tests for arrow creation, label placement, selection, and inspector field coverage.
data.acceptanceTest: Replay worker-to-reviewer and repair-to-living-doc handoffs; inspect each arrow and assert gate/effect/status/source/target/artifact-path fields.
data.proofStatus: Graph/server contracts assert contract_handoff stream events and inspectable edge contract fields including gate, effect, status, source/target paths, and commit intent.

data.name: Graph updates preserve relationship meaning
data.objectiveTerm: how each emitted event changes the graph or lifecycle state
data.criterion: When worker finish, reviewer start, repair ordered, living doc changed, or closure review start events arrive, the dashboard updates the correct existing node or edge rather than redrawing from a hard-coded sequence.
data.proofRequired: Incremental event replay test with before/after graph state assertions for node identity, edge identity, labels, and statuses.
data.acceptanceTest: Replay an event sequence and assert stable node ids across status updates plus creation of only the expected new edges.
data.proofStatus: Contract fixtures assert stable artifact-derived nodes/edges across repair and closable shapes; realtime UI contract proves a streamed graph_update adds the reviewer node and worker-to-reviewer edge while preserving selected active-unit behavior.

data.name: Backend persists enough event history for reload recovery
data.objectiveTerm: active lifecycle runs and selected detail panes
data.criterion: If the dashboard reloads during or after a run, it reconstructs the current lifecycle graph, active/completed unit states, contracts, and recent log tail from persisted local artifacts.
data.proofRequired: Persisted event history or artifact snapshot plus browser/API test that reloads mid-run or after replay and reconstructs state without relying on live memory.
data.acceptanceTest: Start fixture stream, apply events, reload dashboard, and assert graph, selected unit fields, contract arrow data, and recent log tail survive.
data.proofStatus: GET /api/lifecycles/:id/events and /graph reconstruct graph, active unit, handoffs, artifacts, log tails, blockers, and local-only privacy from persisted files. The dashboard now loads persisted event history on selected-graph initialization before opening the WebSocket, the realtime UI contract proves restored lifecycle_snapshot, contract_handoff, and worker log history are visible after script load, and the Playwright realtime browser proof passes.

data.name: Implementation stays standalone and local-only
data.objectiveTerm: preserve the existing standalone/local-only boundary and must not depend on the supervising user chat session
data.criterion: The dashboard reads from local .living-doc-runs, evidence/living-doc-harness, and local log/artifact paths only; it does not depend on supervising-chat memory, remote telemetry, or non-local lifecycle state.
data.proofRequired: Code inspection or tests proving all state sources are local harness artifacts and that the stream/recovery path works from files generated by the standalone lifecycle.
data.acceptanceTest: Run dashboard replay from persisted local artifacts in a fresh process and assert the graph and logs render without any supervising-session input.
data.proofStatus: Contract tests assert localOperatorOnly=true and supervisingChatStateIncluded=false for history and WebSocket events; stream source is local-harness-artifacts and tails read local paths only.

data.name: Inference units start with contract-selected MCP/tool profiles
data.objectiveTerm: what MCP/tool profile constrained it and preserve the existing standalone/local-only boundary
data.criterion: Every contract-bound inference unit records the selected toolProfile in its prompt, input contract, result, lifecycle events, and dashboard graph metadata; local-harness and local-repair profiles prevent ambient user-config MCPs or OAuth-backed plugins from being inherited by default while explicitly passing --sandbox danger-full-access so units can perform required local repo writes.
data.proofRequired: Launcher, dashboard, and repair-chain contract tests proving Codex is started with --ignore-user-config, --sandbox danger-full-access, a local living_doc_compositor MCP override, and artifact-visible toolProfile metadata.
data.acceptanceTest: Run node --test tests/contract/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.spec.mjs, node --test tests/contract/living-doc-harness-runner.spec.mjs, node --test tests/contract/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.spec.mjs, and node --test tests/contract/living-doc-harness-repair-skill-runner.spec.mjs; assert worker, reviewer/closure-style units, balance scan, and repair skills carry toolProfile metadata, dashboard lifecycle starts pass executeProofRoutes/toolProfile through, and no unit depends on ambient MCP inheritance.
data.proofStatus: Implemented in scripts/living-doc-harness-tool-profile.mjs, scripts/living-doc-harness-inference-unit.mjs, scripts/living-doc-harness-runner.mjs, scripts/living-doc-harness-dashboard-server.mjs, and scripts/living-doc-harness-repair-skill-runner.mjs. Targeted contract tests pass for inference-unit launch, worker launch contract, dashboard lifecycle start/graph metadata, and repair-skill chain artifacts.

data.name: Dashboard does not claim hidden model reasoning
data.objectiveTerm: showing the right inference log running to see its reasoning
data.criterion: UI labels and code distinguish emitted inference logs, prompts, contracts, tool calls, summaries, stdout, stderr, and artifacts from unavailable hidden model reasoning.
data.proofRequired: UI copy review plus test or code assertion that labels use emitted-log language instead of hidden-reasoning claims.
data.acceptanceTest: Search dashboard source and rendered HTML for prohibited hidden-reasoning claims; inspect selected unit pane labels for explicit emitted-log wording.
data.proofStatus: Dashboard source and graph UI contract keep prompt/raw trace private and label visible evidence as emitted logs, contracts, summaries, artifacts, stdout/stderr, not hidden reasoning.

data.name: Failure and blocker states stream clearly
data.objectiveTerm: lifecycle, inference-unit, contract, graph, artifact, and log events
data.criterion: Unit error, blocker, and lifecycle continuation states stream into the dashboard as inspectable graph and side-pane states without pretending the objective is closed.
data.proofRequired: Fixture or test run that emits a blocker/error event and verifies graph status, side-pane reason, issue/artifact path, and lifecycle state.
data.acceptanceTest: Replay blocker fixture and assert blocked unit, blocker reason, artifact path, and non-closed lifecycle status are visible.
data.proofStatus: Server contract asserts lifecycle_blocked events with blocker reason, graph blocker node, issue link, artifact path, and non-closed continuation state remain visible.

data.name: Live stream is covered by contract tests
data.objectiveTerm: backend must stream lifecycle, inference-unit, contract, graph, artifact, and log events over WebSockets
data.criterion: Automated tests cover event schema, normal-run ordering, replay/recovery, graph updates, selected-unit log selection, and at least one blocker/error path.
data.proofRequired: Contract and UI tests committed with fixtures and commands that fail if the live stream, graph update, log selection, or recovery behavior regresses.
data.acceptanceTest: Run the targeted contract suite for realtime harness dashboard streaming and verify all tests pass locally.
data.proofStatus: Focused dashboard contracts, the realtime UI-runtime contract with reload-history restoration, and the committed Playwright realtime UI test pass in this worker environment. Iteration 2 reran the targeted proof set and npm run test:contract successfully.

sections.title: Event And Contract Model
sections.rationale: Names the event families and contract handoffs that the realtime dashboard must expose without turning WebSockets into a second source of truth.

data.name: Lifecycle state events
data.purpose: Tell the dashboard when a lifecycle starts, resumes, blocks, closes, or records an error.

data.name: Inference-unit state events
data.purpose: Tell the dashboard which inference unit is prepared, running, completed, blocked, or failed.

data.name: Contract handoff events
data.purpose: Tell the dashboard which unit or lifecycle object handed control to the next unit and which contract evidence carries that handoff.

data.name: Log append events
data.purpose: Stream newly available emitted log lines for the selected inference unit without claiming hidden reasoning.
outputs: recent log history for reload recovery

sections.title: Implementation Surfaces
sections.rationale: Keeps the concrete source-system surfaces visible so implementation cannot close on a decorative realtime mockup.

data.name: Dashboard backend server
data.summary: Adds the local WebSocket endpoint, event replay source, lifecycle artifact watchers or emitters, and event schema validation.

data.name: Dashboard graph frontend
data.summary: Consumes persisted event history and streamed events, updates graph nodes and contract arrows in place, preserves selection, and renders active unit state and log panes.

data.name: Lifecycle controller emit points
data.summary: Emits or persists lifecycle, unit, contract, artifact, and log events at the moment they become true during standalone execution.

data.name: Realtime stream contract tests
data.summary: Proves event schema, event ordering, graph updates, selected-unit log routing, reload recovery, and blocker/error paths.
data.proofStatus: Targeted contract tests pass locally. The realtime UI contract exercises the actual dashboard script and proves persisted event-history restoration before live WebSocket updates; the Playwright realtime browser proof and npm run test:contract also pass in this worker environment.

sections.title: Relationship Maps
sections.rationale: Makes the realtime flow inspectable as structure. These maps are not proof; they are anchored projections that acceptance criteria and tests must verify.

data.name: WebSocket event chain
data.claim: A standalone lifecycle run emits persisted local events that the dashboard streams to create a live graph, inspector, and selected-unit log view.
data.semanticRole: Implementation-guiding: prevents WebSockets from becoming a decorative live channel disconnected from persisted harness artifacts.
data.validationQuestion: Can every streamed graph update and log append point back to a local lifecycle, unit, contract, or log artifact path?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the frontend receives live events that cannot be reconstructed after reload or if graph state is invented client-side.

diagrams.title: Realtime Event Chain
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Lifecycle["Standalone lifecycle controller"] --> EventLog["Persisted lifecycle events.jsonl"]
  Lifecycle --> UnitDirs["Inference-unit artifact dirs"]
  UnitDirs --> Logs["unit codex-events / stdout / stderr"]
  EventLog --> Stream["Local WebSocket stream"]
  Logs --> Stream
  Stream --> Graph["Live lifecycle graph"]
  Stream --> Inspector["Selected node/edge inspector"]
  Stream --> LogPane["Selected unit emitted log pane"]
  EventLog --> Reload["Reload recovery"]
  UnitDirs --> Reload
  Reload --> Graph

data.name: Operator inspection loop
data.claim: The operator sees the lifecycle as it moves: active unit, starting contract, emitted log stream, graph relationship, and next handoff all remain tied to the same local artifacts.
data.semanticRole: Operator-surface contract: defines what must be visible for a human to understand a live run without supervising it through chat.
data.validationQuestion: When an operator selects a running unit or a contract arrow, does the side pane show the correct contract and emitted log source for that selected object?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if the dashboard shows a global tail, stale log, or active marker that is not selected from the unit's artifact path.

diagrams.title: Operator Inspection Loop
diagrams.text:
flowchart TD
  Active["Active inference unit"] --> Card["Highlighted graph card"]
  Contract["Input contract path"] --> Card
  Card --> Selection["Operator selects card"]
  Selection --> Pane["Side pane: role, status, contract, artifacts"]
  Selection --> Tail["Tail matching emitted log stream"]
  Handoff["Contract handoff event"] --> Arrow["Graph arrow with label"]
  Arrow --> EdgePane["Side pane: gate, effect, source, target"]
  Pane --> Decision["Operator can see why next state is pending, repaired, blocked, or closed"]
  Tail --> Decision
  EdgePane --> Decision

data.name: Emitted-log reasoning boundary
data.claim: The dashboard makes emitted inference evidence visible, not hidden chain-of-thought or unavailable reasoning internals.
data.semanticRole: Governance: protects the operator surface from overclaiming what the system can inspect.
data.validationQuestion: Do UI labels and tests distinguish emitted logs, contracts, prompts, tool calls, summaries, stdout, stderr, and artifacts from hidden reasoning?
data.driftRisk: The map is stale if UI copy says it shows model reasoning while the source is only emitted logs or summaries.

diagrams.title: Reasoning Visibility Boundary
diagrams.text:
flowchart LR
  Unit["Inference unit"] --> Emitted["Emitted events and logs"]
  Unit --> Artifacts["Prompt, contract, result, validation"]
  Emitted --> Dashboard["Dashboard evidence view"]
  Artifacts --> Dashboard
  Hidden["Hidden internal reasoning"] -. "not available / not claimed" .-> Dashboard
  Dashboard --> Operator["Operator inspection"]

sections.title: Proof Ladder
sections.rationale: Stages the proof required before this objective can close. A connected WebSocket is only the first rung, not completion.

data.name: Event schema and endpoint proof
data.claim: A local WebSocket endpoint emits typed lifecycle events with schema validation.
data.evidenceRequired: Contract tests for endpoint connection, event schema, required event types, and local-only source paths.
data.closureRelevance: Necessary but not sufficient: proves the stream exists, not that the operator surface works.
data.evidenceObserved: Full contract suite and dashboard server spec prove WebSocket schema, explicit required transition event types, graph/contract/artifact/log events, and local-only privacy.

data.name: Live graph update proof
data.claim: Graph nodes, arrows, labels, and statuses update from streamed artifacts without refresh.
data.evidenceRequired: Browser test or replay proof for closable and repair-path lifecycle shapes.
data.closureRelevance: Proves the live dashboard is artifact-derived rather than a static diagram.
data.evidenceObserved: Server and graph contracts prove artifact-derived graph updates and repair/no-repair shapes; realtime UI contract proves persisted history restoration followed by streamed graph_update creates the reviewer card and contract arrow without reload. Playwright realtime browser proof passes.

data.name: Selected inference-unit log proof
data.claim: The dashboard tails the emitted log stream for the selected unit or contract evidence path.
data.evidenceRequired: Two-unit fixture with distinguishable logs and assertions that selection changes the displayed stream.
data.closureRelevance: Proves the operator can inspect the right running unit instead of a global or stale log.
data.evidenceObserved: API tail and fixture markers prove path-bound selected logs; realtime UI contract proves persisted worker log history restores on load, then the displayed log switches from worker-only to reviewer-only markers and drops stale worker content.

data.name: Reload recovery proof
data.claim: The dashboard reconstructs graph, state, contract details, and recent logs from persisted local artifacts after reload.
data.evidenceRequired: Reload test from persisted events and inference-unit artifact directories.
data.closureRelevance: Proves WebSocket state is recoverable and does not live only in frontend memory.
data.evidenceObserved: Persisted event history and graph APIs reconstruct state from files, and the dashboard now loads /api/lifecycles/:id/events during selected-graph initialization before connecting the WebSocket. The realtime UI contract proves lifecycle_snapshot, contract_handoff, and worker log history are visible immediately after script load, and Playwright realtime browser proof passes.

data.name: Blocker and error path proof
data.claim: Blocked and error states stream clearly and remain non-closure unless objective closure or user stop is proven.
data.evidenceRequired: Fixture or run that emits blocker/error events and asserts visible graph, side-pane, and lifecycle state.
data.closureRelevance: Protects against false closure and makes true live failures inspectable.
data.evidenceObserved: Contract tests assert lifecycle_blocked stream events, blocker graph nodes, issue links, artifact paths, and non-closure state.

sections.title: Regression Boundaries
sections.rationale: Records the boundaries that should not be re-litigated during implementation.

data.name: Realtime means local WebSocket over standalone harness artifacts
data.decision: The realtime dashboard is a local operator surface over the standalone harness. It is not remote telemetry and not a supervising-chat UI.
data.reason: The objective depends on the standalone harness boundary: the system must be observable without borrowing state from the user session.
data.consequence: Tests must prove local artifact replay and reload recovery.

data.name: Show emitted logs, not hidden reasoning
data.decision: The dashboard may show emitted inference logs and artifacts, but must not describe them as hidden reasoning.
data.reason: The operator needs real evidence. Overclaiming hidden reasoning access would make the dashboard misleading.
data.consequence: UI copy and tests must use emitted-log language.

data.name: A static graph is not completion
data.decision: A graph mockup, static artifact-derived graph, or manually refreshed graph cannot satisfy this objective.
data.reason: The objective is a realtime operator surface for active runs.
data.consequence: Closure requires streamed graph updates and selected-unit log inspection.
